methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data, including comparisons of protein and lipid concentrations, effects of pollen type and queen source, and gene expression analysis.

steps.description: Average soluble protein and lipid concentrations in spring and fall IAp and AZp were compared using analysis of variance followed by Tukey's or Fishers pairwise comparisons for post hoc analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOVA and post hoc comparisons on protein and lipid concentrations

steps.description: Comparisons of amino and fatty acid concentrations between IAp and AZp (spring and fall) were made using analysis of variance.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOVA on amino and fatty acid concentrations

steps.description: The effects of pollen type and queen source on pollen consumption, protein digestion, total protein consumed, hemolymph protein concentration, and HPG size were determined using a two-way analysis of variance using pollen and queen types as factors in the general linear model.
steps.searchable_summary: perform two-way ANOVA on pollen consumption and related metrics

steps.description: Whether there were seasonal effects on the parameters listed above within the same queen type when fed spring vs. fall pollens was determined using a general linear model with trial and pollen source along with the interaction term as factors in the model.
steps.searchable_summary: use general linear model to assess seasonal effects on parameters

steps.description: An additional analysis was conducted using a multifactorial analysis of variance to compare responses to seasonal pollens between workers from the same queen type using data from spring and fall trials.
steps.searchable_summary: perform multifactorial ANOVA on seasonal pollen responses

steps.description: Gene expression (hex 110, hex 70a and vg) were compared between queens, pollen types and seasons using Kruskal-Wallis tests.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Kruskal-Wallis tests on gene expression data

methods.method_name: Cage Setup, Pollen Feeding and Sampling
methods.description: Procedure for setting up bee cages, feeding them with pollen, and sampling for analysis.

steps.description: Frames of sealed brood from all colonies headed by either IAq or CAq were placed in separate hive boxes according to queen source, and put in a temperature-controlled dark environmental room (32-34°C, 30-40% relative humidity).
steps.searchable_summary: place brood frames in temperature-controlled room

steps.description: Upon emergence, 50 workers from IAq or CAq were placed in Plexiglas cages.
steps.searchable_summary: place emerged workers in Plexiglas cages

steps.description: The pollens were ground into powder with a coffee grinder and mixed thoroughly before feeding them to the bees.
steps.searchable_summary: grind and mix pollen for feeding

steps.description: The ground pollen (10 g) was inserted into a plastic tube positioned on the side of each cage.
steps.searchable_summary: insert ground pollen into feeding tubes

steps.description: Each cage had a bottle with 30 mL of 50% sucrose solution and another with 30 mL of water.
steps.searchable_summary: provide sucrose solution and water in cages

steps.description: Sucrose solution was exchanged with fresh solution on day 4 and 7 to reduce mold growth.
steps.searchable_summary: exchange sucrose solution to prevent mold

steps.description: Pollen consumption was measured by weighing the tube containing pollen as a single unit prior to feeding, and after day 4 and 7 days to estimate consumption.
steps.searchable_summary: measure pollen consumption by weight difference

steps.description: Protein digestion, hemolymph protein concentration and HPG acini size in the spring and fall trials were estimated in the same five bees sampled from each cage on d-7.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate protein digestion and hemolymph concentration

steps.description: The bees were placed in separate vials, and stored at −20°C until analysis.
An additional 10 bees were sampled per cage, placed in separate vials and stored at −80°C until analysis for expression of vg and hex 70, and 110.
steps.searchable_summary: store additional bees at -80°C for gene expression analysis
